faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about MenuLinker. Can't find your answer?
faq.cta_subtitle: The MenuLinker team replies in less than 48 business hours.
faq.a1: MenuLinker has a free forever plan that includes 1 location and 1 menu with up to 30 products and a basic QR code. Paid plans start at €14.99/month (monthly billing) and include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.
faq.q2: How does the digital menu with QR work?
faq.a2: You create your menu from the control panel, organise sections and products, choose a design template and generate your personalised QR code. Your customers scan the QR with their phone camera and access the updated menu instantly. No app download needed.
faq.q3: Can I translate my menu into multiple languages?
faq.a3: Yes, with Business and Enterprise plans MenuLinker automatically translates your menu into English, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Catalan and more. You can review and edit each translation manually to ensure quality. Customers choose the language in the digital menu.
faq.q4: Are allergens displayed on the menu?
faq.a4: Yes. You can assign the 14 mandatory declaration allergens according to EU Regulation 1169/2011 to each dish. Allergens are displayed with clear icons in the digital menu.
faq.q5: Can I change prices and dishes in real time?
faq.a5: Yes, any change you make in the panel (price, availability, description, photo) is immediately reflected across all active QR codes. No need to reprint or regenerate the QR code. Perfect for daily specials or seasonal dishes.
faq.a6: MenuLinker includes 8 design templates for the public menu: from minimalist and modern styles to more classic designs with elegant typography. You can change the template at any time without losing your data.
faq.q7: Can I manage multiple locations from a single account?
faq.a7: Yes. Depending on the plan, you can manage multiple locations from a single panel. Each location can have its own menus, opening hours and QR codes. Perfect for chains or restaurant groups.
faq.q8: How does the paid free trial work?
faq.a8: Pro and Business plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. At the end of the trial period, you can subscribe or automatically switch to the free plan without losing your data.
faq.q9: Can I cancel my subscription at any time?
faq.a9: Yes, with no commitment or penalties. Cancel from the panel at any time and your account switches to the free plan at the end of the already paid period. We will never overcharge you.
faq.a10: Yes. MenuLinker operates in accordance with GDPR. All data is stored on servers within the European Union, with encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest. We perform daily backups.
faq.a11: All plans include email support with a response in less than 48 business hours. Business and Enterprise plans have access to priority support and chat during business hours (Monday to Friday, 9am–6pm CET).
faq.q12: Do I need programming knowledge to use MenuLinker?
faq.a12: No. MenuLinker is designed so that anyone can use it without technical knowledge. Creating your first menu takes less than 5 minutes.

getting_started.intro_body: MenuLinker is a SaaS platform for the hospitality industry that lets you create digital menus accessible via QR codes. Your customers scan the QR at the table and instantly access the updated menu — no app download required.
getting_started.step1_body: Go to the «Locations» section and click «New location». Enter the establishment name, full address and a contact phone number. The location is the base unit — each location can have one or more menus.
getting_started.step2_body: Inside the location, click «New menu». Give it a name (for example, «Summer menu») and choose one of the available design templates. The template defines the visual appearance your customers will see.
getting_started.step3_body: Access the menu and create sections (Starters, Mains, Desserts…). Within each section add dishes with their name, description, price and optionally an image. You can assign allergens and ingredients to each dish.
getting_started.step4_title: Step 4: Generate the QR and share it
getting_started.step4_body: From the menu page, click «Generate QR». Download the high-resolution QR and print it to place on your tables. Every time you update the menu, the QR will automatically point to the latest version.
getting_started.tip: Tip: You can start completely free with the basic plan. No credit card is required to try the platform.

locations.what_title: What is a location in MenuLinker?
locations.what_body: A location represents a physical establishment of your business: a restaurant, a bar, a café or any point of sale. Each location has its own configuration, menus, opening hours and visual identity.
locations.create_body: Go to «Locations» in the navigation menu and click «New location». Fill in the form with the name, address, city and country. Once created, you can complete the rest of the information from the edit page.
locations.fields_body: In addition to the name and address, you can configure: a contact phone number so customers can call directly, opening hours for each day of the week, a main photo displayed on your public website, and brand colours to customise the appearance of your menus.
locations.multi_body: If you have more than one establishment, you can manage them all from the same panel. Business and higher plans allow an unlimited number of locations. Each location maintains its own menus and configuration independently.
locations.tip: Tip: The location photo is displayed on your public website. Use a quality image (minimum 800×600 pixels) to make a great first impression.

menus.what_body: A menu (or card) is the catalogue of dishes your customers will see when they scan the QR. Each menu belongs to a location and has a design template, a name and a set of sections with their dishes.
menus.create_title: Creating a menu and choosing a template
menus.create_body: From the location page, click «New menu». Choose a descriptive name (for example, «Lunch menu» or «Wine list») and select one of the 8 available design templates. The template can be changed at any time without losing the content.
menus.sections_title: Sections: how to organise your menu
menus.sections_body: Sections are the categories of your menu: Starters, Mains, Desserts, Drinks, etc. Create as many sections as you need and add dishes within each one. Sections are displayed in the order you define.
menus.reorder_body: Drag sections to change their order. Customers will see sections in the order you set. This lets you highlight the most important sections, such as daily specials, first.
menus.activate_body: You can have several menus per location and activate or deactivate each one as needed. Only active menus are accessible via QR. This lets you prepare seasonal menus before publishing them.
menus.templates_body: MenuLinker offers 8 design templates to suit different types of business: from elegant designs for fine dining restaurants to informal styles for bars and cafés. Each template is optimised for mobile and includes support for images, allergens and multiple languages.
menus.tip: Tip: You can have several menus active simultaneously per location. For example, a lunch menu, a dinner menu and a wine list, each with its own QR code.

products.add_body: Inside a menu, select the section where you want to add the dish and click «Add dish». Enter the name and, if you wish, a description. Save the dish and you will be able to complete the remaining information from the edit screen.
products.fields_body: Each dish can have: a name, detailed description, price (with local currency support), calories (kcal) and an image. The image is displayed in the digital menu and noticeably improves the customer experience.
products.allergens_body: MenuLinker includes the 14 mandatory declaration allergens according to European regulation. In the editing of each dish, mark the allergens present. This information is displayed prominently in the menu so customers can make informed decisions.
products.ingredients_body: You can add the list of ingredients for each dish. Ingredients are saved in a shared catalogue across all your menus, avoiding duplicates and simplifying management. When you type an ingredient name, the platform will suggest matches from your catalogue.
products.tags_body: Add tags to your dishes to highlight important characteristics: vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, spicy, lactose-free. These tags are displayed with icons in the digital menu, helping customers quickly identify dishes that suit their preferences.
products.catalog_body: With Business and higher plans, you have a centralised catalogue of products and ingredients. From the catalogue you can manage all your dishes globally, assign them to multiple menus at once and maintain consistency across locations.
products.merge_body: If you have duplicate ingredients (for example, «Tomato» and «tomato»), you can merge them from the ingredients catalogue. The merge automatically updates all dishes that used the duplicate ingredients.
products.tip: Tip: The ingredients you enter are automatically suggested when adding new dishes. Keep names consistent to get the most out of the shared catalogue.

translations.how_body: MenuLinker allows you to offer your menu in multiple languages. Each menu has a primary language (the language in which you enter the content) and can have additional languages. Customers will see a language selector in the digital menu.
translations.auto_body: MenuLinker can generate automatic translations of your menu using artificial intelligence. The automatic translation covers the name and description of each dish, as well as section names. The process takes a few seconds per language.
translations.add_body: From the «Translations» section of the menu, click «Add language» and select the language you want to add. You can opt for automatic translation or enter the translations manually. Both options can be combined.
translations.remove_body: To remove a language from your menu, go to «Translations», locate the language and use the delete option. This action deletes all translations for that language for that menu. The primary language cannot be deleted.
translations.selector_title: The language selector in the public menu
translations.selector_body: When your menu has more than one language, a selector automatically appears at the top of the digital menu. Customers can switch language with a tap. The selected language is maintained throughout the session.
translations.tip: Tip: Automatic translations are a good starting point, but it is always worth reviewing them. Specific culinary terms or proper dish names may require manual adjustment.

qr_codes.generate_title: How to generate a QR for your menu
qr_codes.generate_body: From the menu page, click the «Generate QR» button. MenuLinker will automatically create a unique QR code linked to that menu. Once generated, the QR remains active indefinitely.
qr_codes.customize_body: You can customise the colours of the QR to match your business visual identity. It is also possible to include your establishment logo in the centre of the QR. Customised QR codes have a better scan rate when they contrast well with the background.
qr_codes.download_body: Download the QR in high-resolution PNG format (minimum 1000×1000 pixels). This resolution is sufficient for professional printing at sizes up to A5. For larger formats, it is recommended to scale up while maintaining the white border around it.
qr_codes.placement_body: The most effective positions are: on tables (in a stand or laminated), at the entrance door or shop window, on your official website, and on your social media profiles. The more access points, the greater adoption by customers.
qr_codes.tip: Tip: The QR always points to the latest published version of your menu. You do not need to print a new QR every time you update the menu; changes are reflected automatically.

team.roles_body: MenuLinker has two user roles in the tenant panel. The Owner has full access to all features, including billing, settings and user management. The Editor has limited access to menu content.
team.editor_body: An Editor can create and modify menus, sections and dishes. They cannot access billing settings, cannot manage other users and cannot change the global tenant configuration. This is the right role for kitchen staff or floor managers.
team.scope_body: It is possible to restrict an Editor's access to one or more specific locations. This way, if you have several establishments, you can assign a different team to each one without them having access to the menus of other locations.
team.manage_body: From the «Team» section of the panel, click «Invite user». Enter the person's email and select their role. They will receive an invitation email to create their account. From that same section you can edit roles or revoke access at any time.
team.plan_body: Team management is available from the Pro plan onwards. With the basic plan there can only be one user (the Owner). Upgrade your plan from the billing section to add collaborators.

billing.plans_body: MenuLinker offers several plans adapted to different needs: the free basic plan to get started, the Pro plan for businesses that need multiple users and advanced features, and the Business plan for chains or groups with multiple locations and a centralised catalogue. Updated details and prices are available in the plans section.
billing.change_body: Go to «Billing» in the panel and click «View plans». Select the plan you want to change to and confirm. If you upgrade, the change is immediate and the remaining days of the current cycle are prorated. If you downgrade, the change applies at the start of the next billing cycle.
billing.trial_body: All paid plans include a 14-day free trial. During that period you have full access to all the features of the chosen plan. No charge is made until the trial period ends and no card is required to get started.
billing.cancel_body: You can cancel your subscription at any time from the billing section. Upon cancellation, you retain access to the paid features until the end of the billed period. There are no penalties or minimum terms.
billing.stripe_body: Payments are processed securely through Stripe, one of the most widely used payment processors in the world. You will receive automatic invoices by email each time your subscription renews. You can view the payment history from the billing portal.

analytics.what_body: MenuLinker automatically records three types of interactions: menu scans (when a customer opens your menu via QR or direct link), visits to your business public website (home), and QR code downloads from the panel. All data is collected anonymously without tracking cookies.
analytics.metrics_body: The analytics dashboard shows: total menu visits in the last 30 days, daily visits in a bar chart, the top 5 most visited menus, classified traffic sources (QR, WhatsApp, Google, social media, direct access), website visits, QR downloads, hourly distribution and device type breakdown. It also includes a current week vs previous week comparison.
analytics.sources_body: Each visit is classified by its referral source: if there is no referrer (direct access from a scanned QR), it is counted as 'QR'. If the referrer includes 'wa.me' or 'whatsapp', it is classified as 'WhatsApp'. Google accesses are grouped under 'Google', and those from Facebook, Twitter or Instagram under 'Social media'. Everything else is classified as 'Direct'.
analytics.hours_body: The peak hours chart shows the distribution of visits across all 24 hours of the day, aggregated from the selected period data. The three hours with the highest traffic are highlighted in the primary colour for easy identification. This information is useful for scheduling menu updates or communication campaigns at peak traffic times.
analytics.devices_body: MenuLinker classifies each visit into three device categories: mobile, tablet or desktop. Detection is based on the browser user agent. Strings such as 'mobile', 'android' or 'iphone' indicate mobile; 'tablet' or 'ipad' indicate tablet; everything else is classified as desktop. In most establishments, over 80% of visits come from mobile devices.
analytics.plan_body: Full analytics are available on Pro and higher plans. With the free plan you can see the total visit count for your menu, but the detailed breakdown (charts, sources, devices, peak hours and weekly comparison) requires a paid plan. You can view available plans in the billing section.
analytics.tip: Tip: Data updates in real time. To avoid inflating statistics, there is a 30-minute deduplication window: if the same IP visits the same menu more than once within that interval, only the first visit is counted.

website.what_body: MenuLinker includes a public website for your business accessible from a subdomain or from your own custom domain. This page automatically displays your establishment information, your menu and your contact details.
website.activate_body: During the onboarding process you are offered the option to activate your website. If you skipped it, you can activate it at any time from «Settings → Public website». Simply toggle the switch and your page will be visible immediately.
website.template_title: Choosing a business type and template
website.template_body: Select the business type that best describes your establishment (restaurant, bar, café, etc.) and choose a visual template. The template defines the structure and style of your public page.
website.content_title: What is shown on your website
website.content_body: Your public website automatically shows: your location's opening hours, your active menu with the option to view the digital menu, contact details (phone, address) and a location map.
website.branding_body: From the location settings you can configure your brand's primary and secondary colours. These colours are applied to your public website and to the elements of your digital menu, giving a consistent and professional image.

hero.subtitle: Create digital menus with custom QR codes, manage allergens, translate into 7 languages and update prices in real time. No code. No fuss.

features.title: Everything your restaurant
features.subtitle: Tools designed for hospitality, not engineers. No learning curve.

qr.title: Custom QR Codes
qr.description: Generate unique codes with your brand colours. Print them on tables, signage or share them on social media.

templates.title: 8 Design Templates
templates.description: From editorial menus to tasting menus. Pick the one that best represents your restaurant and change it whenever you like.

analytics.title: Real-Time Analytics
analytics.description: Discover which dishes get the most views, at what times and from which devices. Make decisions based on real data.

allergens.title: Allergens & Ingredients
allergens.description: Clear information for your customers. Comply with the European allergen regulation (EU 1169/2011) effortlessly.

realtime.title: Instant Updates
realtime.description: Change prices, add daily specials or deactivate out-of-stock products. Changes appear instantly on the QR.

multilang.title: Automatic Multi-Language
multilang.description: Translate your menu into 7 languages with one click: English, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Catalan and more.

highlight.title: Your menu, beautiful on any device
highlight.description: Customers scan the QR and see a mobile-optimised digital menu. No apps, no installs. Just scan and enjoy.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent plans
pricing.subtitle: No mandatory annual contracts. Cancel whenever you want.
pricing.bottom_note: All plans include hosting, updates and email support.

maria.content: We used to print the menu every week because of price changes. Now we update it from our phone in 30 seconds. The savings are incredible.

carlos.content: Foreign customers really appreciate being able to see the menu in English or French automatically. The multi-language feature is a clear differentiator.

ana.content: The analytics surprised me. I discovered that my croquettes are what people look at most but not what I sell the most. With that data I changed the presentation and it improved.

cta.title: Ready to digitise
cta.subtitle: Create your digital menu in minutes. No commitments, no credit card. Join more than 500 restaurants that already trust MenuLinker.

footer.brand_description: Digital menus with QR for hospitality. Designed for restaurants that want to stand out.
